message:
Welcome to The Unknown Unknown, are you prepared for the challenge?

 *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands* 
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Hey team, getting a few reports now, looks like confusion from the customers on what they ordering?
This is strange
Just seen a couple of reports of customers not receiving the right item on delivery
Hmm? that sounds strange. @utopkara-bloomberg.net can you take point and start sizing this up?
sounds good
*Incident Number INC100091:* customers not receiving the right item on delivery sev-3
:star: Incident record created
How big is this problem?
:bulb: Understand the problem by reproducing it
@bob can you pull up the tickets for the customer complaints
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Checking reports...
is there a pattern in the reports?
One report said the user wanted to buy 'Sunglasses' but he got 'Tank-top' in confirmation
Just double checked with shipping looks like the items that has been ordered are correct and we're sending the right item
We have currently got more than 17 complaints so far
Hi @Umut , are teh customers able to place orders?
Have all 17 received the items they have ordered correctly?
I think , right in thier confirmation emails, they are getting wrong product than they ordered
This is a bit confusing
Gathering more information....
The customers are complaining that the image is not matching up with the product name
Seems to be that users don't know whether the product they're purchasing matches the image or the description.
I think teh confirmation itself is wrong
We'll likely see high return rates from customers, that's going to mean a lot of financial loss from shipping and refund costs.
@daniel please can you check the logs of the 17 tickets and investigate the mismatch
:star: Manage actions and timings
I tried to place orders , i am able to place with otu any issue
I'm receiving more cancellation/refund requests than new orders. @utopkara-bloomberg.net What shall we do now?
is there a problem , can you try please
I'm concerned we might be doing more harm than good by letting the new orders continue...
I can see that the item image is not matching the item picked in the cart
Oh hoo, i get it
:star: Qualify the problem
Orders are still coming in high volume, and we now have 130+ customers asking to cancel their orders
@Umut, what product are the customer buying , One on label or image
*Incident Number INC100071:* sev-2
We could try to halt all incoming orders but not sure on the scale of impact
@utopkara-bloomberg.net Could you please discuss this with Bez?
There is a problem with the cart images
We have escalated to sev-2
In that case, we are taking wrong orders
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
This may have a huge financial impact
If we're taking the wrong orders, then let's pause all incoming orders right away. We can't afford huge returns or refunds
Will update as we have more details, there are more than 170 incidents so far
What's the impact?
@daniel please can you check the latest releases from today, and find out whether there is a possible cause
Sure, i will take a look at that ,
:star: Check changes done to the platform
do we want to make sure that we dont take any new wrong orders?
Here's the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Hope this helps:https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MDI1YjRiNzUxMGEwNGVmMjkwNmU2Y2ZkNzRjZjBjMWE
@hamed please can you check the databases, and whether there is anything that would cause the references images to be incorrect
:star: Know your team and their roles
I will do that @Umut - we are taking wrong orders, do you think its worth putting banner or maintenance window so we dont take any new orders ?
@daniel  looks like there are some changes to the cart from today
Yes, its safe to rollback, do you want us to rollback
@hamed the problem seems to be with the cart view not the orders
@Umut- we are taking wrong orders, i woudl suggest we stop the bleeding and bring a maintence window
yes please roll back asap
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
No, i see th eissue in main page as wel
we've identified the possible cause
all th eimage a nd prodyct name is messe dup
OK, let's pull the banner up
I am rolling back now
I have rolled back the checkout out change
@bez we'll pull the banner up as you've suggested
i still see the issue
can you check
@hamed need you to check the databases
what has changed between 10 mins ago and now?
Sure, i will check - Lets prioritize the maintenance page, @Dan can you do it
Whenever needed, we can switch the site to maintenance mode via a feature-flag update in the config repo. It cleanly pauses new orders once business gives the go-ahead
Do we see any errors in the logs ?
Thanks
@bez just to be clear, is it OK to put the banner up?
Getting up maintenance banner on the website...
Yes please, go ahead
I dont see any error in any apps
I got the approval from Bez to put the banner up now
same here , the platform looks okay as well
Ok the website now displays a maintenance banner
@utopkara-bloomberg.net How does it look now?
The site doesn't load for me
:star: Reproduces the incident
@utopkara-bloomberg.net Now that we are under maintenance. Let's prioritize resolution
I don't see the maintenance banner
the site doesn't load
It should be on
OK, I see it now
The issue could be anywhere, we should look at the configuration mapping between product images and names.
@Daniel do you know which application holds this data?
all right, who can have a look at the mapping?
@Tanya,can you check if this is stored in a database or Redis cache?
@hamed @daniel ?
@utopkara-bloomberg.net if there are no visible errors, this might be a configuration issue; the above details may help narrow it down.
The configuration is stored in the Product Catalogue Service. I pushed a change in UAT yesterday that updates the Redis cache with the latest product names.
@tanya @hamed were there any configuration changes rolled out for the platforms today?
@Hamed yes, this information is stored in Redis cache and the cache was refreshed earlier today.
Dan had one on Redis cache in UAT
Can you send me a photo of what you are seeing wrong with the platform @utopkara-bloomberg.net
@daniel please can you roll back the change to the configuration?
Whats the latest ? @Umut
let's bring the site state to the way it was yesterday
I can check the database and provide a Redis key dump for product:<id>:name keys
'Redis key dump' means extracting the stored data for specific Redis keys like 'product:<id>:name' so you can see what values are currently saved in the Redis database.
Ok here is what i found
@Umut, checking that
But product_id: 3 is actually 'mug'
Where are we in the investigation? I just want to know the current working theories
It is, looks like my script has run in PROD and updated the wrong names for each product in DB
Oh no, that not good! Shall we go with a rollback?
@daniel what is the best way to roll back?
Its a DB change, rollback might no thelp
Redis is a cache, so perhaps we can invalidate the entries and let it fill up again?
I think this is cache issue we should clear the cache and restart Product catalogue Service, that will make fresh calls to DB and the cache will be refreshed
:star: Form a working theory
No, Dan's query has updated the wrong names in DB. The data is wrong in DB therfore we will have to rehydrate the canonical database with the correct product Id names .
Do we have an ETA for the fix?
@Tanya, i think you are complicating an easy solution. A restart should fix this
It your call @utopkara-bloomberg.net
@hamed please can I get your review on the issue?
Sure
lets review, i think the change was implemented on PROD instead of UAT
that mean, the DB has been updated or populated with wrong data
the question is: is the database corrupted with the wrong values
Yes, its a DB corrupt, cache refresh might not help
we should go with tanya's option
OK, let's restore the DB from its last known good state
@tanya please restore the database
We have data stored in canonical DB
what is the ETA?
We will have to follow below steps to Rehydrate the db.
'Rehydrate the db' means restoring or repopulating the main database with accurate data from a reliable source or backup.
1. **Flush** corrupted product-name keys

```bash
redis-cli --scan --pattern 'product:*:name'| xargs redis-cli del```
2. **Rehydrate** product name cache from canonical DB
3. **Restart** frontend/product-catalog-service** to refresh in memory cache
4. **Verify** product listings display correct names
I can perform Step 1 and 2. Dan or Shay has to perform step 3

we found a possible issue; the production database has been corrupted, we are restoring the database
will get back with an ETA
@utopkara-bloomberg.net please verify that all steps have been completed
Are we good to proceed @utopkara-bloomberg.net?
@daniel please can I get your help with 3
Should take around 5 to 10 minutes
Sure, i am ready
@daniel how long will your part take?
Shoudl be less than 2 minutes
thanks
Shall we go ahead?
Thanks, i am waiting
we are restoring the state of the database, the whole operating should take somewhere between 7 to 12 minutes, I'll get back with an update
Perfect
yes
Thanks for go ahead, implementing step 1 now
Step 1 is now complete
Moving on to step 2
Step 2 complete
lets proceed with the next 2 steps
Starting step 3.....
@hamed please can you verify Tanya's work by checking the database entries
:star: Cross checking resolution with team
Sure, i ll check the DB
Step 3 is complete
All the recovery steps are completed
@daniel how can we check if the site has recovered
Ill check the app mapping , may be Tanya can check in DB mapping before we bring down the maintenance site
@tanya please can you verify the database entries are correct
@utopkara-bloomberg.net Checked the mapping again. And this how it looks now
Surfe
Always confirm service health, errors, and data testing before removing a maintenance page - it avoids surprises when traffic returns
OK, the database entry seems to be correct
I have validated every product  mapping - this looks alright now
@daniel is there a way we can see the site without bringing down the maintenance banner?
No, but i see data is correct now
OK, let's bring the maintenance banner down and quickly check
Maintenance page removal in progress...
We are LIVE for orders back again!
@tanya @hamed @shay please help with trying out the page
go
We've recovered, but a few orders are in dispute. Should we cancel them or contact customers first?
Please confirm this with Bez before we proceed
Checking
Sure, ill check as wel
I still see the maintenance banner
Will take 30 seconds
can you check now
we are bringing the site back to see if the changes have worked
Great - Keep me posted
will update asap
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
I see it is alright , mapping is accurate
i can place orders as well
@bez the site is back up, working correctly
Appreciate it , it was tricky
business is asking for clarification on what to do with the orders placed during the problem
If a customer really wants to cancel, go ahead - otherwise leave the order as is. For anything not delivered yet, have support double-check with the customer before taking action
Looks like we are back to business

Bez's decision:
If a customer really wants to cancel, go ahead - otherwise leave the order as is. For anything not delivered yet, have support double-check with the customer before taking action
thans'
Well done everyone
Root cause - UAT data refresh script was accidentally run against prod, overwriting product names in DB and Redis cache with test data. Monitoring missed it as health metrics stayed normal.
Thanks @utopkara-bloomberg.net and team. You kept the Online Boutique on track - great work under pressure
Thanks @Umut
@daniel @hamed please could you help write a memo for post-mortem
ill be carefull next time
